name: CALM 1.2 Node Types Reference
description: A reference architecture demonstrating all 9 CALM 1.2 core node types using realistic financial services industry (FSI) naming. This file exists to teach the vocabulary — each node illustrates a distinct node-type with a description of its role. Not a production system.

nodes.name: Trading Analyst
nodes.description: A human user who initiates trading activity by accessing the trader portal. Represents an external entity whose actions trigger system interactions but who does not own infrastructure.

nodes.name: Production Kubernetes Cluster
nodes.description: The cloud runtime environment (AWS EKS) into which trading services and databases are deployed. Other nodes are deployed-in this ecosystem — it is the platform, not a service.

nodes.name: Trade Execution Platform
nodes.description: The logical system boundary encompassing all services, databases, and components required to process a trade from submission to settlement. Composed-of the services that fulfil trade execution.

nodes.name: Order Service
nodes.description: A running microservice that receives order submissions, validates them against risk limits, and routes them to the exchange gateway. Exposes a REST API over HTTPS.

nodes.name: Trades Database
nodes.description: PostgreSQL relational database persisting submitted trade orders, execution records, and settlement status. Accessed by the Order Service via JDBC.

nodes.name: Edge Firewall
nodes.description: Layer 4 network infrastructure that inspects and filters inbound traffic from the public internet before it reaches the trader portal. Represents L3/L4 network infrastructure, not a deployment environment.

nodes.name: Corporate LDAP Directory
nodes.description: Active Directory-based identity provider that stores employee authentication credentials and group memberships. Other services depend on this node for identity resolution — it is infrastructure, not an initiating actor.

nodes.name: Trader Portal
nodes.description: Browser-based web application through which trading analysts monitor positions, submit orders, and review execution history. Serves as the front-door UI for the trade execution platform.

nodes.name: End-of-Day Position File
nodes.description: Structured CSV data asset containing aggregated end-of-day position snapshots exported for downstream risk and reporting systems. Represents data at rest — a file, not a running service.

relationships.description: Trading analyst accesses the trader portal to submit and monitor orders
Trader portal calls the Order Service REST API to submit and retrieve orders — traffic passes through the edge firewall first
Order Service reads and writes trade records to the Trades Database
Order Service authenticates users and resolves group memberships via the Corporate LDAP Directory
Order Service writes end-of-day position snapshots to the positions data asset for downstream risk reporting
The Order Service and Trades Database are deployed-in the Production Kubernetes Cluster — the cluster is the runtime ecosystem
The Trade Execution Platform system is structurally composed-of the Order Service — the system boundary wraps its constituent services
The Trader Portal traffic passes through the Edge Firewall on its way to backend services
